title: Capabilities & Fit
subtitle: What a Sentinel can watch, expose, and deliver — and whether it fits your use case.
intro: AppySentinel is a boilerplate — the capabilities below are the building blocks. Each project uses the ones it needs. Some are proven in live pilots; others are designed and ready to build.

status_legend.label: Available now
status_legend.detail: Proven in a real project or ready to deploy

status_legend.label: Planned
status_legend.detail: Specified and designed — built on demand for your project

zones.name: Collect
zones.question: What sources can a Sentinel watch?
zones.intro: The Sentinel reaches out and gathers data on a schedule — or listens for data arriving. Inbound only.

capabilities.name: Remote server fleet
capabilities.detail: Poll any number of remote machines via SSH. Nothing installed on the remote servers — the Sentinel sends commands and reads results out.

capabilities.name: Database by watermark
capabilities.detail: Poll a database for records that changed since the last check. Keeps a local mirror in sync without full table scans.

capabilities.name: HTTP / API endpoints
capabilities.detail: Poll any HTTP endpoint on a configurable interval. Works with any REST API.

capabilities.name: File system events
capabilities.detail: Watch a directory for files being created, changed, or deleted. Triggers on change — no polling interval required.

capabilities.name: Incoming webhooks
capabilities.detail: Receive POST events from external systems. The Sentinel listens; external systems push.

capabilities.name: Local shell commands
capabilities.detail: Run any local command on a schedule and capture its output as data.

zones.name: Access
zones.question: How does your code or AI agent talk to it?
zones.intro: The Sentinel exposes collected data through one or more surfaces. You choose which ones your project needs.

capabilities.name: MCP server — AI agents
capabilities.detail: Exposes data as MCP tools that Claude and other AI agents can call natively. The primary surface for AI-forward workflows — your agents get structured data without any custom plumbing.

capabilities.name: REST API — dashboards and scripts
capabilities.detail: A simple HTTP interface any dashboard, browser, or script can query. Endpoints for health, latest signals, and snapshots.

capabilities.name: CLI — terminal and automation
capabilities.detail: Query the Sentinel from a terminal. Pipe-friendly — compose with jq, grep, or any shell tool.

zones.name: Deliver
zones.question: Where can it push data outward?
zones.intro: Optionally push collected data to other systems on a schedule — without being asked.

capabilities.name: HTTP endpoint
capabilities.detail: Batch-push data to any remote endpoint with retry and backoff.

capabilities.name: Supabase
capabilities.detail: Push data directly into a Supabase table — useful for cloud-accessible storage or sharing across projects.

capabilities.name: File sync
capabilities.detail: Relay collected data to another machine on the same network via rsync or Syncthing.

fit_guide.heading: Is AppySentinel a fit?
good: You want AI agents to query your data without hitting production systems
You need a single view across a fleet of machines
You want a data mirror that stays fresh automatically
You're building a dashboard that needs live data from multiple sources
You want AI-native data access (MCP) without building a custom server
not: You need a cloud-hosted, fully managed solution
You need to write back to the systems you're watching — it is read-only by design
You're looking for a point-and-click, no-code setup
